figure.title: TX Master Key Rollover Protocol
figure.purpose: Details the complex, multi-step protocol for rolling over the Master Key on the TX side.

technical_summary: Triggers when the sum of epoch counters for all 3 TX streams hits the 'Master key expiry threshold'.
Switches to the alternate master key and derives new keys for all 3 streams with {0x0, StreamID} as salt.
Initiates a handshake with the remote RX using 'KeyRollMSG.ReqChannel'.

Sequentially switches the key for the request stream, then coordinates with RX for the read response stream ('KeyRollMSG.RdRspChannel'), and finally for the write response stream ('KeyRollMSG.WrRspChannel').
Ensures both sides synchronize the switchover for each specific stream before proceeding.

steps: Handshake and switch Read Response stream.
Handshake and switch Write Response stream.

critical_facts: Master key rollover is a synchronized, stream-by-stream handshake process between TX and RX to ensure no data is lost or decrypted with the wrong key during the transition.
